Finland.description: 30% up to 30,000 EUR, 34% on the exceeding amount. Assuming here that we only sell over 10 year old investments

Finland for 2 people.description: 30% up to 30,000 EUR, 34% on the exceeding amount- so 60keur with 30% with two people. Assuming here that we only sell over 10 year old investments

Italy_7_Percent.description: Flat 7% tax regime for retirees moving to specific southern regions.

Italy_General.description: Standard 26% capital gains tax. Wealth tax ignored per requirements.

Spain_Madrid_Andalusia.description: Progressive savings tax rates (19% to 28%). Assumes residency in Madrid or Andalusia to bypass the Spanish Wealth Tax.

Portugal_General.description: Standard 28% flat rate on capital gains. Wealth tax ignored.

Czech_Republic.description: 0% if held for over 3 years (Time Test). Otherwise 15%. Assumed long-term hold.

Bulgaria.description: Flat 10% income/capital gains tax. Note: Sales of UCITS ETFs on EU regulated markets are technically 0%.

Finland.description: Flat 30% Source Tax (Lähdevero) on domestic bank account interest.

Finland for 2 people.description: Flat 30% Source Tax (Lähdevero) on domestic bank account interest.

Czech_Republic.description: Flat 15% withholding tax on domestic bank interest. Unlike investments, there is no 3-year time test exemption.

Italy_General.description: Flat 26% final withholding tax on standard bank and postal accounts.

Italy_7_Percent.description: Flat 7% applies to foreign-sourced bank interest under the southern retiree regime.

Spain_Madrid_Andalusia.description: Interest goes into the general savings base and stacks with capital gains using the same 19-28% progressive brackets.

Portugal_General.description: Flat 28% withholding tax on standard bank deposits.

Bulgaria.description: 0% tax on bank interest from EU/EEA accounts (tax-free as of 2022).

Finland.description: Estimated progressive tax rate for pension income (State + Municipal + YLE + Health).

Italy_7_Percent.description: Flat 7% applies to foreign pensions in designated southern municipalities.

Spain_Madrid_Andalusia.description: General progressive income tax (IRPF). Combined State + approximate Madrid regional rates.

Portugal_General.description: Standard Portuguese IRS progressive brackets for pensions (Non-NHR).

Czech_Republic.description: Pensions are tax-exempt up to 36x the minimum wage (approx €28k). Excess taxed at flat 15%.

Bulgaria.description: Flat 10% income tax applies to pensions.
